title: Novel Haptic Electronic Yarn for Wearable Textiles: Vibrotactile Feedback via Knitted Channels for Skin-Based Communication
pdf_version_label: 暂无可合法自动获取的开放全文

abstract.original: Abstract This work introduces a novel electronic yarn (E-yarn) capable of delivering controlled vibrotactile feedback, offering a seamless haptic integration technique into wearable textiles. By embedding miniature vibrotactile motors at the yarn level and employing robust encapsulation techniques, the design ensures durability and compatibility with everyday wear. Prototypes, including a knitted glove incorporating these haptic E-yarns, demonstrated sustained functionality over 20 wash cycles with minimal resistance change ( −2 , with only 1–5% frequency variation and up to 40% amplitude increase post-integration to wearables. This technology enables reliable haptic feedback within textiles, paving the way for future applications in assistive communication and interactive wearables.
abstract.zh: 提供机器人、可穿戴或电子皮肤系统任务证据；可用于低离散/装配容差触觉界面的结构与对照设计。摘要可核实数值包括：5%、40%。
